channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bitcoin tops $100,000 as big rally sparked by Trump election win rolls on
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin has topped the $100,000 mark as a massive rally in the world's most popular cryptocurrency sparked by the election of Donald Trump rolls on.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Women and people of color still face workforce inequality, reports show
item.description: Even though women represent the majority of the population, they are still under-represented in the workplace.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Police hunt for UnitedHealthcare CEO's masked killer after 'brazen, targeted' attack on NYC street
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A gunman killed UnitedHealthcare’s CEO on Wednesday in a “brazen, targeted attack” outside a Manhattan hotel where the health insurer was holding its investor conference, police said, setting off a massive search for the fleeing assailant hours before the annual Rockefeller Center Ch
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, KAREN MATTHEWS and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Police say searchers don’t expect to find woman in Pennsylvania sinkhole alive
item.description: UNITY TOWNSHIP, Pa. (AP) — The search for a woman who is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole in western Pennsylvania has become a recovery effort after two treacherous days of digging through mud and rock produced no signs of life, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GENE PUSKAR and MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge considers first lawsuit to overturn Missouri's near-total abortion ban
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Abortion-rights advocates on Wednesday asked a judge to overturn Missouri’s near-total ban on the procedure, less than a month after voters backed an abortion-rights constitutional amendment. Jackson County Circuit Judge Jerri Zhang did not immediately rule after the hearing.
item.dc:creator: SUMMER BALLENTINE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California Christian school shooting wounds 2 boys, 5 and 6; suspected gunman dead
item.description: Two California private school students, ages 5 and 6, were shot Wednesday, and the suspected gunman is dead, according to authorities and reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Search intensifies for missing Pennsylvania grandmother as crews encounter unstable mine shafts
item.description: The search for Pennsylvania grandmother Elizabeth Pollard continued on Wednesday. Pollard, 64, is believed to have fallen into a sinkhole on Dec. 2 while searching for her pet.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Memphis police use excessive force and discriminate against Black people, Justice Department finds
item.description: The Memphis Police Department uses excessive force and discriminates against Black people, according to the findings of a U.S. Department of Justice investigation launched after the beating death of Tyre Nichols after a traffic stop in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Adrian Sainz And Jonathan Mattise, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Two boys, ages 5 and 6, shot at religious school in Northern California; suspect dead
item.description: A suspected gunman was killed after a shooting at Feather River School of Seventh-Day Adventists in Palermo, Butte County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Underground voids from abandoned mines pose danger to people and property when land collapses
item.description: Swaths of Pennsylvania and many other states are honeycombed with old, unstable mines that can cause the earth to suddenly give way.
item.dc:creator: Michael Rubinkam And Matthew Brown, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Supreme Court seems likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on medical treatments for transgender minors
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Hearing a high-profile culture-war clash, the Supreme Court on Wednesday seemed likely to uphold Tennessee's ban on gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court Latest: Majority of justices appear likely to uphold state ban on transgender care
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments on Wednesday in just its second major transgender rights case, a challenge to a Tennessee law that bans gender-affirming care for minors.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: If you had a Patreon account, you may qualify for this payment
item.description: Patreon will pay $7.25 million across users who had accounts and accessed videos on Patreon.com between April 1, 2016 and Sept. 23, 2024.
item.dc:creator: Katie Millard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump selects Jared Isaacman to lead NASA
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced he picked Jared Isaacman to lead NASA in his incoming administration.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: An ex-officer who died in an apparent suicide before his federal trial was barred from having a gun
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — A former police detective in Kansas who died in an apparent suicide as he was about to stand trial on civil rights violations for allegedly sexually assaulting and terrorizing vulnerable women for decades wasn't supposed to have a gun while he was under house arrest.
item.dc:creator: JOHN HANNA and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trooper, the dog abandoned on I-75 before Hurricane Milton, finds forever home
item.description: Trooper, the dog who was rescued by the Florida Highway Patrol during Hurricane Milton has now been adopted, the Leon County Humane Society announced.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Shoplifter inspires new clothing line from Southern California boutique
item.description: After a Southern California store was targeted by a shoplifter, the owner decided to feature the thief as the star of his latest clothing collection.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KRON4

item.title: UnitedHealthcare CEO fatally shot in 'targeted attack' in NYC, officials say
item.description: The CEO of UnitedHealthcare was killed in a Midtown shooting Wednesday morning, sources have confirmed to Nexstar's WPIX.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, Fox 5

item.title: British indie rock band robbed at gunpoint in California '10 minutes into US tour,' group says
item.description: Sports Team, a six-piece London-based alternative rock band, had stopped for coffee when someone alerted them that their van was being ransacked by robbers, according to their latest Instagram post.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, Fox 5

item.title: No winner for Mega Millions, jackpot grows to $579 million
item.description: No one won the Mega Millions drawing on Tuesday night. The jackpot has now grown to an estimated $579 million ahead of the next drawing on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Knight, ABC 4

item.title: Spotify Wrapped: How to view, share your personalized recap
item.description: Want to show everyone who your top five artists were or just how much time you spent listening to music and podcasts? Now is your chance.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KRON4

item.title: Oregon prosecutor slams 'leniency' of state law protecting man convicted of 13-year-old girl's rape, murder
item.description: A prosecutor is criticizing an Oregon law that would significantly reduce prison time for a man, 18, was sentenced to life for the rape and murder of a 13-year-old girl.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missouri executes a man for sexually assaulting and strangling a 9-year-old girl in 2007
item.description: BONNE TERRE, Mo. (AP) — Rowan Ford knew Christopher Collings as “Uncle Chris” after he spent several months living with her family. On Tuesday, Collings was executed for sexually assaulting and killing the child, then dumping her body in a sinkhole outside a small Missouri town.
item.dc:creator: JIM SALTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: App claims its AI can predict when you'll die
item.description: The app also features a countdown clock that features your death day and a picture of the Grim Reaper.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, KRON4

item.title: US closes investigation into E. coli outbreak linked to onions in McDonald's Quarter Pounders
item.description: The U.S. government said Tuesday it has closed its investigation into an E. coli outbreak tied to McDonald's Quarter Pounder hamburgers after determining there is no longer a safety risk. The outbreak, which was first reported Oct.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN and JONEL ALECCIA, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Justice Department announces sweeping reforms to curb suicides in federal prisons and jails
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department is ramping up efforts to address a crisis of suicides and systemic failures in federal prisons and jails, announcing sweeping reforms aimed at overhauling how mental health care is provided behind bars.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL BALSAMO and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Sinkhole may have swallowed Pennsylvania grandmother looking for missing cat: police
item.description: Authorities fear a Pennsylvania grandmother searching for her missing cat may have been swallowed by a sinkhole.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, WGN 9

item.title: California teacher dies after suspected bat bite in her classroom
item.description: A central California middle school teacher died after contracting rabies from a suspected bat bite, local health officials said.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, Fox 5

item.title: Trump says he would block US Steel acquisition by Nippon Steel
item.description: The Japanese firm announced last year that it would acquire U.S. Steel Corp. in a $14.9 billion deal — a move that was sharply criticized by lawmakers on both sides of the aisle.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, KRON4

item.title: GM recalls over 130,000 trucks over unexpected tailgate unlatching
item.description: Over 132,000 Chevrolet and GMC trucks are facing a recall over concerns that the tailgate may unlatch unexpectedly.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Hallenbeck, Fox 5

item.title: Taco Bell adding photo booth experience to drive-thrus nationwide for a limited time
item.description: Select Taco Bell drive-thrus nationwide will be transformed into a “one of a kind” photo booth experience to capture fans’ everyday “Live Mas” moments. Taco Bell rewards members in California, Ohio, Tennessee, Texas and Florida will be able to participate in the drive-thru photo booth experience.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Law enforcement leaders react to Trump selecting Hillsborough Sheriff Chad Chronister as DEA Administrator
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump announced Saturday afternoon he's nominating Hillsborough County Sheriff Chad Chronister to be the next Administrator of the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA).
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecution makes closing argument in murder trial over stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Closing arguments began on Monday in the trial of a tech consultant in the 2023 stabbing death of Cash App founder Bob Lee in San Francisco, an event that shocked the broader tech community whose members mourned the loss of an entrepreneur they called charismatic and kind.
item.dc:creator: JANIE HAR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: US Postal Service touts timely delivery of mail ballots despite concerns from election officials
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service said Monday nearly 100% of completed mail ballots were returned to election offices within a week for this year's presidential contest, despite hurricanes, some misdirected election mail and delivery concerns raised by state officials.
item.dc:creator: DAVID SHARP and STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What is 'lake-effect snow'? Warm air from large bodies of water is the key ingredient
item.description: When towns along the Great Lakes get buried in drifts of blowing snow, like several have over the past few days, weather experts start talking about the “lake effect." Lake-effect snow often occurs in relatively narrow bands that dump copious amounts of snow.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The Coast Guard suspends its search for the crew of a capsized fishing boat in the Gulf of Alaska
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — The search for five people believed to be aboard a fishing vessel that capsized in rough seas in the Gulf of Alaska has been suspended, the Coast Guard said Monday. The search lasted nearly a day and covered more than 108 square nautical miles (370 square kilometers).
item.dc:creator: MARK THIESSEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Marshall Brickman, who co-wrote 'Annie Hall' with Woody Allen, dies at 85
item.description: The Oscar-winning screenwriter Marshall Brickman, whose wide-ranging career spanned some of Woody Allen 's best films, the Broadway musical “Jersey Boys” and a number of Johnny Carson's most beloved sketches, has died. He was 85.
item.dc:creator: JAKE COYLE, Associated Press, Fox 5
